results.possibleIdentity: Rapheal O. Diaz (also referred to as Raphael O. Diaz) was a materials engineer and technical expert at the Westinghouse Electric Corporation during the late 1960s and early 1970s. Based in East Orange, New Jersey, his professional expertise encompassed metallurgy, physical chemistry, semi-conductor materials, thermoelectrics, and liquid crystals. He was a member of the Technical Services Program of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to foster collaborations between the scientific and artistic communities. Within E.A.T., engineers like Diaz were matched with artists based on their specific technical skills; his background in liquid crystals and metallurgy was particularly relevant during a period when Westinghouse was developing specialized technologies like Spectratherm—a liquid crystal product used in artistic performances and medical thermography. His affiliation with E.A.T. is documented through the organization's technical matching files, which utilized a McBee Keysort index card system to categorize member engineers by their location and technical fields.
